questions.question: What is a Dockerfile?
options: A running container that serves an application
A text file with instructions for building a Docker image
A binary executable that Docker uses internally
A log file generated when a container starts
questions.answer: A text file with instructions for building a Docker image

questions.question: Which Dockerfile instruction specifies the base image to build upon?
questions.answer: FROM

questions.question: What does the WORKDIR instruction do in a Dockerfile?
options: Sets the working directory inside the container for subsequent instructions
Changes the directory on the host machine
Specifies which directory to exclude from the build
questions.answer: Sets the working directory inside the container for subsequent instructions

questions.question: Which instruction copies files from the host machine into the Docker image?
questions.answer: COPY

questions.question: What does the RUN instruction do during a Docker build?
options: Starts the container after the build finishes
Executes a command during the image build process
Defines the default command when a container starts
Runs a health check on the container
questions.answer: Executes a command during the image build process

questions.question: What is the purpose of the CMD instruction in a Dockerfile?
options: It executes a command during the build process
It specifies the default command to run when a container starts
It copies files into the image
questions.answer: It specifies the default command to run when a container starts

questions.question: What does the EXPOSE instruction do?
options: Opens a port on the host machine automatically
Documents which port the application inside the container uses
Blocks all network traffic to the container
Maps a host port to a container port
questions.answer: Documents which port the application inside the container uses

questions.question: Which instruction sets environment variables in a Dockerfile?
questions.answer: ENV

questions.question: In the command 'docker build -t my-app:1.0 .', what does the '-t' flag do?
options: Sets a timeout for the build
Tags the image with a name and version
Runs the build in test mode
questions.answer: Tags the image with a name and version

questions.question: In the command 'docker build -t my-app:1.0 .', what does the '.' at the end represent?
options: The build context — the directory containing the Dockerfile
questions.answer: The build context — the directory containing the Dockerfile

questions.question: What base image is used in the assignment's simple Python Dockerfile?
questions.answer: python:3.12-slim

questions.question: What does the '--rm' flag do in 'docker run --rm hello-docker:1.0'?
options: Runs the container in read-only mode
Automatically removes the container when it exits
Removes the image after the container stops
Restarts the container if it crashes
questions.answer: Automatically removes the container when it exits

questions.question: How do you pass an environment variable to a container at runtime?
questions.answer: docker run -e APP_USER="Campbell" image-name

questions.question: Why is requirements.txt copied and installed BEFORE copying app.py in the Flask Dockerfile?
options: Because app.py depends on requirements.txt at build time
So Docker can cache the dependency installation layer and skip it if only app.py changes
Because COPY instructions must be in alphabetical order
Because requirements.txt is larger and must be copied first
questions.answer: So Docker can cache the dependency installation layer and skip it if only app.py changes

questions.question: What does the '--no-cache-dir' flag do in 'pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt'?
options: Forces pip to reinstall all packages
Prevents pip from saving downloaded packages to a cache, reducing image size
questions.answer: Prevents pip from saving downloaded packages to a cache, reducing image size

questions.question: In 'docker run -d -p 5000:5000 --name my-flask flask-app:1.0', what does the '-d' flag do?
options: Runs the container in detached mode (in the background)
Deletes the container after it stops
questions.answer: Runs the container in detached mode (in the background)

questions.question: In '-p 5000:5000', what does the first '5000' represent?
questions.answer: The host port

questions.question: Which command is used to view the logs of a running container named 'my-flask'?
questions.answer: docker logs my-flask

questions.question: What base image is used for the static website Dockerfile in the assignment?
questions.answer: nginx:alpine

questions.question: Where must you copy an HTML file to serve it with the default Nginx configuration?
questions.answer: /usr/share/nginx/html/

questions.question: What does it mean when Docker says 'CACHED' during a rebuild?
options: The layer failed and Docker is retrying
Docker is reusing a previously built layer that has not changed
The image is being downloaded from Docker Hub
The layer is being compressed to save space
questions.answer: Docker is reusing a previously built layer that has not changed

questions.question: If you modify app.py but not requirements.txt, which layers are rebuilt?
options: Only the layers after the point where app.py is copied
Only the RUN pip install layer
No layers — the entire image is cached
questions.answer: Only the layers after the point where app.py is copied

questions.question: Which command lists all Docker images on the local machine?
questions.answer: docker images

questions.question: What is the correct sequence to stop and remove a container named 'my-site'?
options: docker rm my-site && docker stop my-site
docker stop my-site && docker rm my-site
questions.answer: docker stop my-site && docker rm my-site

questions.question: What does each instruction in a Dockerfile create in the resulting image?
questions.answer: A new layer
